intro: The clearest difference between Cline and Void is pricing — Cline is freemium (has a free tier), while Void is free. Beyond that, lock-in — Cline has low lock-in (OSS VS Code agent, BYOK models), whereas Void has low lock-in (OSS VS Code fork). Cline fits accelerating coding workflow; Void fits privacy-conscious developers who want to bring their own LLM keys and avoid SaaS middlemen. On the strengths side, Cline is cited for accelerating coding workflow, while Void is cited for open-source Cursor alternative with no middleman backend. The honest trade-off: Cline is the wrong call for non-coding roles; Void is the wrong call for users wanting the most polished agent experience or built-in team features; Cline users flag non-coding roles, and Void users flag features lag Cursor and Windsurf by a meaningful margin. Choosing between them should come down to the constraint that actually binds your workflow, not surface-level overlap.
quick_take: Cline is for accelerating coding workflow; Void is for privacy-conscious developers who want to bring; decide based on pricing model.
verdict_a: Choose Cline if your use case is accelerating coding workflow, and a freemium (has a free tier) pricing shape fits your budget better than free.
verdict_b: Choose Void if your use case is privacy-conscious developers who want to bring their own LLM keys and avoid SaaS middlemen, and a free pricing shape fits your budget better than freemium (has a free tier).

faq.a: On listed pricing Void is free, whereas Cline follows a freemium model, so Void is cheaper on the sticker. Factor in self-hosting and maintenance time before concluding which is actually cheaper for your team. Check both pricing pages for current tiers.

faq.q: Can I migrate from Cline to Void?
faq.a: Migrating from Cline to Void is feasible when their scopes overlap, but the work is in re-mapping configuration, data models, and integrations. Cline has low lock-in, so exporting state is typically straightforward. Plan for a dual-run period so you can validate parity before decommissioning.

Cline and Void score the same on developer experience (4/5 each) in our data. Choose based on which workflow, docs style, and CLI ergonomics feel closer to your team's preferences. Trial both in a small project before committing.

faq.q: Is Void a good alternative to Cline?
faq.a: Void is a reasonable alternative to Cline when Void fits privacy-conscious developers who want to bring their own LLM keys and avoid SaaS middlemen. If your use case matches Cline's sweet spot — accelerating coding workflow — staying with Cline is probably safer. Cline itself is not meant for non-coding roles, which is often where users start shopping for alternatives.
